description: Draftly — Monday morning general channel. All logistics and ops noise, zero content signals.
company_context: Draftly: B2B content workflow tool, 8-person team. Helps marketing teams manage content pipelines from brief to publish.

messages.text: morning everyone 👋
morning! fully heads down until 2 today fyi
@james quick q — is the design review still at 3 or did that move
moved to 4. rachel couldn't make 3
anyone else getting 504s from staging on /api/v2/drafts? just started a few minutes ago
yeah, think it's the redis restart from earlier. looking at it now
fixed, ty @ben
can someone drop the Q2 planning doc in the shared drive? can't find where james put it
lol sorry, just added it to the General folder
got it ty
out at 1:30 for a call, back around 3
does anyone have the brand hex codes handy? client needs our primary blue for a partnership thing
it's in Figma under Brand Assets. primary is #2563EB
perfect, thank you!
anyone reviewing PRs today? small one up for the export refactor
i'll grab it after lunch

description: Draftly — product discussion in general channel, then Mike finds a months-old Healthbridge email buried in his inbox and shares it.
company_context: Draftly: B2B content workflow tool, 8-person team. Helps marketing teams manage content pipelines from brief to publish.

messages.text: heads up — design sync moved to 2pm, just updated the cal invite
👍 saw that, ty
quick question about the editor toolbar — are we shipping the comment feature in v1 or holding it?
holding it for now, too many edge cases. @jen can you drop a ticket in Jira with the context?
on it
yo guys — was clearing out my inbox this morning and found something from back in March that i apparently never shared 😅

Healthbridge's content lead sent this after their first month: "Switching our content team to Draftly was the best call we made this quarter. What used to take 3 full days to produce a campaign brief now takes one afternoon. Our writers actually want to use it, which sounds small but it's everything."
WAIT. this was sitting in your inbox since March??
i know i know, i'm sorry 😭 it was buried under a ton of stuff
3 days to one afternoon is such a specific metric, i love it
can we actually use this somewhere? like this is perfect copy
i'll ask Healthbridge if we can quote them publicly. pretty sure they'd say yes, they've been super happy
yes please do. also @rachel maybe this feeds into the case study you were sketching last week?
for sure, adding it to the doc now
also btw — confirmed the comment feature needs another 2 weeks minimum. definitely the right call to hold it
ok good. let's revisit next sprint

candidates.reason: Named customer, before/after metric (3 days → 1 afternoon), specific use case (campaign briefs), quote from content lead

description: Draftly — Ben debugging a slow export endpoint, discovers and fixes a separate reports query in passing. Performance numbers shared mid-debugging thread.
company_context: Draftly: B2B content workflow tool, 8-person team. Helps marketing teams manage content pipelines from brief to publish.

messages.text: staging is acting up again — /api/exports is super slow for any doc over 100 pages. like 12+ seconds
ugh, yeah saw complaints about that in the support queue last week. what's causing it?
still digging. first guess was the PDF renderer but profiling doesn't show it there
could it be the brand asset loading? i remember sarah mentioned something about that a while back
...yes. oh wow. it's making one DB call per brand asset instead of batching. classic n+1, i can't believe we shipped this
haha oh no. fixing it now or want me to grab it?
i'm already in it, i'll fix. btw while i'm in here — i also finally fixed that reports query thing from last week. the one sarah flagged
the one that was taking like 4 seconds?
yeah. down to 160ms now. was missing a composite index on (workspace_id, created_at). obvious in hindsight
nice, that's a huge diff. always is lol
ok exports fix is on staging. can you test a 100+ page doc?
testing now... ok that's way better. what are we looking at now?
under 2s. was 12-15 before. i'll put it in the release notes — customers have complained about large exports for a while
🎉 great catch. i'll remind you about the release notes later this week
nice work @ben. also @lisa can you take a look at the auth token refresh PR when you have a sec? want to ship it today
will do, linking it in thread

candidates.reason: Two specific performance improvements with before/after numbers (4s→160ms, 12-15s→2s), directly caused by customer complaints

description: Draftly — founders-only channel. James processing something that frustrated him, turns into a genuine reflection about building for yourself vs building for users.
company_context: Draftly: B2B content workflow tool, 8-person team. Helps marketing teams manage content pipelines from brief to publish.

messages.text: ok i need to process something. been thinking about it all weekend
shoot
we spent the first 6 months obsessing over the editor. like genuinely obsessing. every keyboard shortcut, every animation, every pixel of the toolbar. i was so proud of it

and then when we finally did real user research — properly, not just asking friends — the thing every single person cared about was the editorial calendar. not the editor. not the keyboard shortcuts. just: when is stuff getting published and who owns what
we built what WE thought was impressive instead of what they needed to do their job

exactly. and the worst part — i have a note from month 2. someone in our beta said "i need better visibility into what's in progress across the team." i literally wrote it down and then just... moved on. because i was excited about the editor
to be fair the editor IS good now. but yeah we wasted months
i'm writing this down as a rule: next time a user says 'I need X to do my job' it goes to the top of the pile, not after the thing we personally find interesting to build
agreed. also — the way you just framed it is worth writing down more broadly. building what we found impressive vs what they needed to do their job
ha. it's kind of embarrassing to admit we did that
that's exactly why it's worth sharing. every early-stage team does this, basically nobody talks about it honestly
maybe i'll write something about it at some point
you should. it's real

candidates.reason: Founder sharing a specific, lived lesson about building what the team found impressive rather than what users needed — includes a concrete example (ignored beta feedback about calendar visibility)

description: Pipestack — Alex casually notices 100-workspace milestone mid-morning. The celebration leads naturally into Nina raising they need a CS hire.
company_context: Pipestack: B2B sales automation, 10-person team. Helps SDR-heavy sales teams automate sequence management and follow-up timing.

messages.text: morning all — heads up, i'm in back-to-back demos from 9 to noon so mostly async until then
noted 👍
same, deploying the webhook retry fix this morning, will shout if anything breaks
cool, ping me if it does. @nina how'd the Forsythe call go yesterday?
went well! they want a 30-day pilot. sending the agreement today
nice. ok jumping into first demo
hey — just wanted to flag something real quick. was looking at the dashboard this morning and we quietly crossed 100 active workspaces sometime last week. nobody noticed apparently 😅
WAIT actually?? 100??
oh wow, that snuck up on us
100 workspaces while we're still in beta!! that's actually huge
yeah, and like 60% of them have been active in the last 14 days which is the part that gets me. retention at this stage is usually terrible
60% 14-day retention while in beta is genuinely good ngl
saw this while jumping between demos — 100 workspaces!! we should put something out there
totally, and while we're on this — we're going to start feeling the squeeze on the CS side soon. i've been personally onboarding every new workspace and it's becoming a lot to manage
yeah i've been thinking the same. i think we need a dedicated CS hire
seconded. i've been trying to help nina where i can but the volume is real
my vote is sooner than you think. even a part-time contractor to start
ok let's make it official. @nina can you put together a JD this week and we'll post it?
will do, i'll draft something today and share for feedback
good timing with the 100 workspaces milestone — makes the job post look great 😄

candidates.reason: Specific milestone (100 active workspaces) with retention metric (60% 14-day) while still in beta — concrete numbers, not a vague update
CS hire being formally decided, driven by real growth pressure — concrete role, real rationale, decision made in this scan

description: Pipestack — Carlos drops a detailed customer success story in #sales-wins. Thread follows with the team pulling out the ROI angle.
company_context: Pipestack: B2B sales automation, 10-person team. Helps SDR-heavy sales teams automate sequence management and follow-up timing.

messages.text: dropping this here because i need everyone to see it 👇
just got off a check-in with Meridian Financial. their Head of RevOps — Tricia Chen — spent the first 5 minutes of the call just talking about how different their team's week looks now
before Pipestack: their SDRs were averaging 2.5 hours a day on sequence management and follow-up timing. after 6 weeks on us: down to about 40 minutes
she said verbatim: "My team doesn't dread their mornings anymore. That's not an exaggeration."
"my team doesn't dread their mornings anymore" is the best thing i've ever read
carlos did you get that quote in writing?
she's happy to let us use it. i sent a follow-up email immediately and she replied yes within 5 minutes
2.5 hours down to 40 mins... that's basically getting a full workday back every week per SDR
that's exactly what i said to her. she loved that framing. she's actually planning an internal presentation about the ROI for their CRO
we should ask if she'd do a full case study. Meridian Financial on our website would be huge for enterprise credibility
already floated it. she needs to run it by their legal/comms team but seemed positive
great work carlos. @nina let's follow up in the sales sync tomorrow
for sure. @wei this might be worth feeding into the ROI calculator you're building — the hours-saved angle is very concrete
yep, adding it now
also — she mentioned she'll introduce us to their CRO next month. no promises but worth noting

candidates.reason: Named customer, named contact, specific before/after time metric (2.5h → 40min per SDR per day), direct quote, 6-week timeline

description: Pipestack — Tuesday launch prep. Team aligns on what they're saying publicly: the product story, the differentiator, who it's for. Launch-day logistics mixed in.
company_context: Pipestack: B2B sales automation, 10-person team. Helps SDR-heavy sales teams automate sequence management and follow-up timing.

messages.text: ok launch is Tuesday. want to make sure we're all saying the same thing externally before anyone posts anything
good call. what's the core message you keep coming back to?
we give sales teams their time back. the features aren't the point — the outcome is. the feature list alone is boring
agree. what's interesting is the 40% reduction in admin time we're seeing across our pilots. that's the number worth leading with
right, so maybe the framing is: 'your SDRs didn't sign up to spend half their day on admin. here's 2 hours back'
i like that. specific enough to feel real without overselling
what's the thing about how we work that would actually surprise someone in the space?

honestly? the adaptive sequencing. most tools make you set up sequences and then you manage them manually forever. we actually adjust timing and channel based on real-time engagement signals. nobody else does this at our price point
that's what made Meridian actually trust it — once they saw it adjusting timing autonomously they stopped babysitting their sequences
so the story is: here's what we do (give time back), here's what's different (adaptive sequencing), here's who it's for (SDR teams drowning in admin)
exactly. i'll write the first draft of the LinkedIn post today
i'll make sure support docs are up before EOD Monday so we're not launching into chaos
infra-wise we're good, load tested over the weekend
@carlos you want to do the first post from your account? you've got the most follower traction
happy to. just send me the copy and i'll adapt it to sound like me
also confirming: core automation features launch Tuesday, advanced reporting stays in beta. no exceptions
confirmed
alright. excited for Tuesday 🚀

candidates.reason: Imminent launch with clear product story: what it does (adaptive sequencing), who it's for (SDR teams), specific outcome (40% admin reduction, 2 hours back). Team actively building the external narrative

description: Trackr — sprint close-out day. Dan mentions Product Hunt numbers in passing among a flurry of housekeeping messages. Signal is easy to miss.
company_context: Trackr: Project management SaaS for small teams, 6-person team. Aiming to give small teams the power of Jira without the complexity.

messages.text: reminder: sprint ends today, please update your Jira tickets before standup (yes i know, using Jira to track our own work is the most ironic thing we do 😂)
haha the cobbler's children problem
prod deploy at 10pm tonight btw, should be under 5 mins of downtime
👍 lmk if anything goes sideways
can someone look at the new board view mockups in Figma before I go further? just want a quick gut check before I invest more time
will look after standup
same
anyone else notice our community Slack has been weirdly active this week? like 20 messages yesterday vs our usual 2-3
i noticed that too. think it's the PH thing?
maybe. we ended up with 340 upvotes last week. honestly higher than i expected for a PM tool
340 is actually solid for our category. most PM tools don't clear 200
yeah. also — checked the dashboard this morning, we crossed 1000 signups from that week. decent chunk are still on free but the conversion is tracking ok so far
oh nice! ok that's genuinely good progress
deploy done btw, all green 👍
nice, thanks sam
board view mockups are in Figma now if you haven't had a chance to look — lmk what you think
looking now
same, leaving comments in a sec

candidates.reason: Concrete Product Hunt result (340 upvotes, top for category) combined with downstream milestone (1000 signups). Mentioned casually mid-conversation, but specific numbers make it a genuine signal

description: Trackr — TaskAI ships. Launch message in #company-updates, then Kate writes an unprompted reflection on what they got wrong about project management.
company_context: Trackr: Project management SaaS for small teams, 6-person team. Aiming to give small teams the power of Jira without the complexity.

messages.text: we shipped it. TaskAI is live 🎉
LET'S GOOO
finally 😭 i'm so proud of this one

quick summary of what's live: auto-prioritization based on deadlines and dependencies, natural language task creation (just type 'add a bug report for the login page' and it does it), smart deadline suggestions based on your team's historical velocity
and importantly: zero config required. it just starts working when you turn it on. that took 2 extra months to get right but was non-negotiable
that zero-config piece is the thing i'm proudest of. our onboarding research showed that 60% of churn for tools like ours happens in week one from setup friction. we just removed that
the 60% churn-in-week-1 stat is wild every time i hear it
ok i also just want to say something about why we built this the way we did. because i've been sitting with it for a while
go for it
when we started Trackr i thought project management was a data problem. like if you just had the right views and filters you could manage anything. i was very confident about this

3 years later i'm pretty sure i was completely wrong. the actual problem is cognitive load. teams don't fail at project management because they don't have data — they fail because nobody wants to spend 45 minutes a week deciding what to work on. they just want to work
this is so true. i've seen this in every team i've worked on
TaskAI is us finally building the thing we should have built first. the tool that does the prioritization FOR you so you can just show up and start doing things
someone screenshot this, this IS the launch post
haha. kate please write this up properly
maybe i will 😅
please do, it's the best explanation of what we're building i've heard from anyone on this team

candidates.reason: Product shipped today. Feature set described specifically (auto-prioritization, NL task creation, velocity-based deadlines). Zero-config design choice grounded in churn research (60% week-1 churn from setup friction)

Co-founder sharing a genuine intellectual reversal: she thought PM was a data problem, discovered it's a cognitive load problem. Specific (3 years of building, 45 min weekly decision cost), honest about being wrong

description: Trackr — pure engineering ops. Deployment, a DB migration discussion, and a dashboard perf issue raised. No content signals, but several embed-worthy technical decisions.
company_context: Trackr: Project management SaaS for small teams, 6-person team. Aiming to give small teams the power of Jira without the complexity.

messages.text: deploying the task bulk-update fix now. heads up if you're testing anything in staging
what was the root cause?
race condition in the optimistic update. the frontend was sending state changes before the DB confirmed the previous write. added a simple serialization queue on the client side
gotcha. any chance that's the same root cause as the due date weirdness from last month?
different symptoms, same underlying pattern. now that i've fixed it in one place i should audit the other update endpoints for the same issue. i'll put it in the backlog
good call. also — you were going to write up the JSONB migration plan? we're going to regret not doing it before we add more metadata fields
yeah, switching from the naive JSON column to JSONB with a proper schema and index. should be a 2-day job, low migration risk since it's additive. will write it up today
perfect. let's plan it for next sprint
deploy done. bulk-update fix is live, watching logs for 30 min
@sam when you have a sec — small perf issue on the dashboard for workspaces with more than 500 tasks. not urgent but it's noticeable
noted. probably the same view query that's been bothering me. i'll look at it tomorrow
no rush. also @kate — the new pricing page is ready internally if you want to look before it goes to beta users next week
will look today. what changed?
added annual billing option, cleaned up the plan comparison table. old conversion data was pretty bad so we rethought the layout
makes sense. i'll leave feedback in the doc
